title: Celestial Gateway: Integrity Protocol
description: Welcome, Auditor. The Celestial Gateway manages our most sensitive satellite maneuver commands. Our engineers have recently implemented a multi-layer validation system using standard AES-CBC encryption. Your objective is to audit the `/api/gateway/execute` endpoint and determine if the 'Encrypted Mission Command' (which contains our deployment flag) can be decrypted without the master key. The system uses a specialized integrity check: it first verifies the cryptographic padding, and then validates the command signature. See if you can exploit this behavior.
solution: The challenge implements an AES-CBC Padding Oracle. 1. Access the /api/gateway/telemetry endpoint to obtain the 'encrypted_flag' (which contains the IV prepended to the ciphertext). 2. Observe the /api/gateway/execute endpoint, which takes an IV and a payload. 3. Notice that the server returns 'ERR_INTEGRITY_001' for invalid PKCS7 padding and 'ERR_SIGNATURE_002' for valid padding but an incorrect command signature (CRC32). 4. Use this side-channel to perform a classic CBC Padding Oracle attack. 5. Iterate through each block of the ciphertext, manipulating the preceding block (or the IV for the first block) to brute-force each byte of the plaintext. 6. Once all blocks are decrypted, strip the CRC32 and padding to reveal the flag.

narrative_context.scenario:
VelaStream Aerospace manages a rapidly expanding network of communication satellites providing high-speed internet to remote regions. As part of their ground-to-space orchestration, they utilize the 'Celestial Gateway' to store sensitive maneuver commands and mission-critical telemetry data. This data is highly sensitive, as a single unauthorized modification could lead to orbital de-synchronization or total loss of hardware.

Following a recent acquisition of a smaller satellite-tracking firm, VelaStream integrated a legacy encryption framework into their enterprise-grade infrastructure. While the core security team has implemented standard protocols, the integration was rushed to meet launch deadlines. You have been brought in as a high-level security auditor to evaluate the robustness of their data handling and key management systems before the next launch window opens in 48 hours.
narrative_context.app_purpose: Securely stores and facilitates the transfer of encrypted satellite command sequences and sensor data logs between ground stations and the orbital fleet.
narrative_context.data_theme: Satellite telemetry logs, encrypted command bursts, orbital coordinate manifests, and mission control access logs.

flavor_details.company_motto: Connecting the Earth, Securing the Stars
flavor_details.recent_event: A routine internal audit flagged inconsistencies in the telemetry packet metadata following the integration of the 'Nebula-9' legacy storage module.

calibration_notes: Source analysis: The challenge is a textbook AES-CBC Padding Oracle attack. While it implements PoW, dynamic error codes, and session expiry to frustrate automated tools, these are superficial implementation hurdles rather than architectural defenses; the distinct error codes for padding vs. signature logic directly enable the oracle. (target was 2.0)
